# WI-QUIC-CRT-0031 - QUIC CRT client-side ServerHello branch-point work item

## Summary

Classify the PSK-capable ClientHello attempt at ServerHello as accepted or rejected on the managed client/runtime path, keep rejection on the existing full-handshake path, and leave resumed-handshake success out of scope.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0133

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0031

## Planned Changes

- The bridge parses the ServerHello `pre_shared_key` selection signal and classifies the PSK-capable ClientHello attempt as accepted or rejected.
- Rejected attempts continue through the existing full-handshake path and do not corrupt the dormant client snapshot.
- Accepted attempts are recognized but stop before any abbreviated-handshake success claim.
- Focused requirement-home tests cover accepted/rejected classification, fallback, early-data closure, and the public no-resumption/no-early-data guard.
- Resumed-handshake success, 0-RTT packet processing, transfer, retry, key update, and any public API widening remain out of scope.

## Out of Scope

- public API widening
- resumed handshake support
- 0-RTT packet send/receive support
- anti-replay behavior
- broad support for arbitrary post-handshake TLS messages
- fake replay or fake resumption success paths
- transfer enablement
- retry enablement
- key update
- broader session-management infrastructure
- opportunistic refactors unrelated to the ServerHello accept/reject branch seam

## Verification Plan

Run focused requirement-home tests for rejected-branch fallback, accepted-branch recognition, bootstrap-without-dormant-material, early-data closure in both branches, and the public no-resumption/no-early-data guard; run the current CRT/API/INT guards and the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep; run repo-local SpecTrace validation and render checks after the canonical JSON updates.

## Completion Notes

This is the landed client-side ServerHello branch-point slice. The managed client/runtime path now classifies a PSK-capable ClientHello attempt as accepted or rejected at ServerHello, cleanly returns rejected attempts to the existing full-handshake path, and recognizes accepted attempts without claiming resumed-handshake success or any public early-data promise.
